proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide have a different objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_enzalutamide.
Patients receiving treatment_abiraterone have a different objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_abiraterone.
Patients receiving treatment_docetaxel have a different objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_docetaxel.
Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a different objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_olaparib.
Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma have a different objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_lu177_psma.
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a different objective_response rate than patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate 0.361 on treatment_enzalutamide (n=20076) vs 0.159 off (n=29924). Diff=+0.202, chi2 p=0.00e+00.
Response rate 0.238 on treatment_abiraterone (n=14991) vs 0.241 off (n=35009). Diff=-0.003, chi2 p=4.70e-01.
Response rate 0.240 on treatment_docetaxel (n=15187) vs 0.240 off (n=34813). Diff=-0.000, chi2 p=9.14e-01.
Response rate 0.239 on treatment_olaparib (n=5098) vs 0.240 off (n=44902). Diff=-0.001, chi2 p=8.86e-01.
Response rate 0.243 on treatment_lu177_psma (n=7504) vs 0.240 off (n=42496). Diff=+0.003, chi2 p=6.00e-01.
Response rate 0.239 on treatment_pembrolizumab (n=2384) vs 0.240 off (n=47616). Diff=-0.002, chi2 p=8.76e-01.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with mcrpc=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with mcrpc=0.
Patients with visceral_mets=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with visceral_mets=0.
Patients with brca2_mutation=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with brca2_mutation=0.
Patients with ar_v7_positive=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with ar_v7_positive=0.
Patients with msi_high=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with msi_high=0.
Patients with psma_high=1 have a different objective_response rate than patients with psma_high=0.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate 0.154 in mcrpc=1 vs 0.346 in mcrpc=0. Diff=-0.192, chi2 p=0.00e+00.
Response rate 0.240 in visceral_mets=1 vs 0.240 in visceral_mets=0. Diff=-0.001, chi2 p=8.71e-01.
Response rate 0.150 in brca2_mutation=1 vs 0.250 in brca2_mutation=0. Diff=-0.101, chi2 p=5.43e-56.
Response rate 0.160 in ar_v7_positive=1 vs 0.260 in ar_v7_positive=0. Diff=-0.101, chi2 p=1.90e-98.
Response rate 0.176 in msi_high=1 vs 0.242 in msi_high=0. Diff=-0.066, chi2 p=2.97e-09.
Response rate 0.239 in psma_high=1 vs 0.243 in psma_high=0. Diff=-0.004, chi2 p=3.05e-01.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with lower objective_response (negative coefficient in logistic regression).
Higher gleason_score is associated with a different objective_response rate.
Older age_years is associated with a different objective_response rate.

analyses.result_summary: Logistic regression coef=-0.2851 (OR per 1-unit ECOG increase = 0.752), p=9.53e-73.
Logistic regression coef=+0.0053 (OR per 1-unit Gleason increase = 1.005), p=6.07e-01.
Logistic regression coef=+0.0002 (OR per 1-yr = 1.000), p=8.20e-01.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher psa_ng_ml (continuous) is associated with a negative change in the log-odds of objective_response (univariable logistic per 1 SD).
Higher albumin_g_dl (continuous) is associated with a positive change in the log-odds of objective_response (univariable logistic per 1 SD).
Higher ldh_u_l (continuous) is associated with a negative change in the log-odds of objective_response (univariable logistic per 1 SD).
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo (continuous) is associated with a negative change in the log-odds of objective_response (univariable logistic per 1 SD).

Higher alt_u_l (continuous) is associated with a positive change in the log-odds of objective_response (univariable logistic per 1 SD).
Higher total_bilirubin_mg_dl (continuous) is associated with a negative change in the log-odds of objective_response (univariable logistic per 1 SD).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic regression of objective_response on all 6 treatments, all biomarkers, ECOG, Gleason, age, and all 16 lab covariates, multiple predictors show independent associations with response — most notably treatment_enzalutamide (positive), and mcrpc/brca2_mutation/ar_v7_positive/msi_high/ecog_ps/weight_loss_pct_6mo (negative).

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ all_treatments + all_biomarkers + age + ecog + gleason + 16_labs)
analyses.result_summary: Top 12 most significant terms in the joint model: treatment_enzalutamide: coef=+1.2013 (OR=3.324, p=0.00e+00); mcrpc: coef=-1.1256 (OR=0.324, p=0.00e+00); ar_v7_positive: coef=-0.6942 (OR=0.499, p=1.14e-108); ecog_ps: coef=-0.3275 (OR=0.721, p=1.14e-82); brca2_mutation: coef=-0.7353 (OR=0.479, p=5.04e-64); weight_loss_pct_6mo: coef=-0.0364 (OR=0.964, p=1.67e-33); msi_high: coef=-0.4308 (OR=0.650, p=2.54e-09); albumin_g_dl: coef=+0.1268 (OR=1.135, p=1.80e-08); psa_ng_ml: coef=-0.0010 (OR=0.999, p=3.25e-08); crp_mg_l: coef=-0.0073 (OR=0.993, p=8.63e-08); ast_u_l: coef=-0.0017 (OR=0.998, p=7.39e-02); potassium_meq_l: coef=-0.0392 (OR=0.962, p=8.10e-02)

proposed_hypotheses.text: Olaparib provides greater benefit in BRCA2-mutated patients (positive interaction on objective_response).
Pembrolizumab provides greater benefit in MSI-high patients (positive interaction on objective_response).
Lu177-PSMA provides greater benefit in PSMA-high patients (positive interaction on objective_response).
AR-V7 positivity reduces or abolishes the benefit of enzalutamide (negative interaction on objective_response).
AR-V7 positivity reduces or abolishes the benefit of abiraterone (negative interaction on objective_response).

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ treatment_olaparib * brca2_mutation + ecog_ps + age + albumin + ldh + hgb + visceral + mcrpc)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction treatment_olaparib:brca2_mutation: coef=-0.2765 (OR=0.758), p=5.99e-02. Treatment main coef=+0.0212; biomarker main coef=-0.6531.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ treatment_pembrolizumab * msi_high + ecog_ps + age + albumin + ldh + hgb + visceral + mcrpc)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction treatment_pembrolizumab:msi_high: coef=+0.0721 (OR=1.075), p=8.18e-01. Treatment main coef=-0.0190; biomarker main coef=-0.4319.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ treatment_lu177_psma * psma_high + ecog_ps + age + albumin + ldh + hgb + visceral + mcrpc)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction treatment_lu177_psma:psma_high: coef=-0.0648 (OR=0.937), p=2.90e-01. Treatment main coef=+0.0629; biomarker main coef=-0.0230.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ treatment_enzalutamide * ar_v7_positive + ecog_ps + age + albumin + ldh + hgb + visceral + mcrpc)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction treatment_enzalutamide:ar_v7_positive: coef=-1.3282 (OR=0.265), p=1.24e-101. Treatment main coef=+1.3981; biomarker main coef=-0.0281.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ treatment_abiraterone * ar_v7_positive + ecog_ps + age + albumin + ldh + hgb + visceral + mcrpc)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction treatment_abiraterone:ar_v7_positive: coef=+0.0718 (OR=1.074), p=2.74e-01. Treatment main coef=-0.0367; biomarker main coef=-0.6724.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within brca2_mutation=1 patients, treatment_olaparib increases objective_response.
Within brca2_mutation=0 patients, treatment_olaparib affects objective_response.
Within msi_high=1 patients, treatment_pembrolizumab increases objective_response.
Within msi_high=0 patients, treatment_pembrolizumab affects objective_response.
Within psma_high=1 patients, treatment_lu177_psma increases objective_response.
Within psma_high=0 patients, treatment_lu177_psma affects objective_response.
Within ar_v7_positive=1 patients, treatment_enzalutamide increases objective_response.
Within ar_v7_positive=0 patients, treatment_enzalutamide affects objective_response.
Within ar_v7_positive=1 patients, treatment_abiraterone increases objective_response.
Within ar_v7_positive=0 patients, treatment_abiraterone affects objective_response.
Within visceral_mets=1 patients, treatment_docetaxel increases objective_response.
Within visceral_mets=0 patients, treatment_docetaxel affects objective_response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Confirmed in a joint model that for each treatment the top three modifier features from the screen retain significance after mutual adjustment, identifying which treatment-by-feature interactions are robust.

analyses.result_summary: Joint adjusted model for treatment_enzalutamide with top-3 modifiers (mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation): treatment_enzalutamide:C(mcrpc)[T.1]: coef=-2.2003, p=0.00e+00; treatment_enzalutamide:C(ar_v7_positive)[T.1]: coef=-1.6180, p=3.77e-137; treatment_enzalutamide:C(brca2_mutation)[T.1]: coef=-1.5380, p=2.72e-66

proposed_hypotheses.text: There exists a clinically defined subgroup (e.g., by mcrpc, visceral_mets, brca2_mutation, ar_v7_positive, msi_high, psma_high, or ecog_ps) in which treatment_abiraterone increases objective_response.
There exists a clinically defined subgroup (e.g., by mcrpc, visceral_mets, brca2_mutation, ar_v7_positive, msi_high, psma_high, or ecog_ps) in which treatment_docetaxel increases objective_response.
There exists a clinically defined subgroup (e.g., by mcrpc, visceral_mets, brca2_mutation, ar_v7_positive, msi_high, psma_high, or ecog_ps) in which treatment_olaparib increases objective_response.
There exists a clinically defined subgroup (e.g., by mcrpc, visceral_mets, brca2_mutation, ar_v7_positive, msi_high, psma_high, or ecog_ps) in which treatment_lu177_psma increases objective_response.

There exists a clinically defined subgroup (e.g., by mcrpc, visceral_mets, brca2_mutation, ar_v7_positive, msi_high, psma_high, or ecog_ps) in which treatment_pembrolizumab increases objective_response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within non-mCRPC patients alone, treatment_enzalutamide produces a substantially larger absolute increase in objective_response than in the overall population.
Within non-mCRPC AND AR-V7-negative patients, treatment_enzalutamide increases objective_response by more than +0.50 in absolute terms versus non-treated patients.

Within non-mCRPC AND AR-V7-negative AND BRCA2-negative AND MSI-low patients (the enzalutamide-responsive subgroup), treatment_enzalutamide raises objective_response from approximately 0.17 to approximately 0.80 — an absolute increase of about +0.63.

Outside the enzalutamide-responsive subgroup (i.e., patients with mCRPC OR AR-V7+ OR BRCA2+ OR MSI-high), treatment_enzalutamide produces no clinically meaningful improvement in objective_response (absolute difference < 0.01).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a joint adjusted logistic model, each of mcrpc, ar_v7_positive, brca2_mutation, and msi_high independently and significantly suppresses (negative interaction) the treatment_enzalutamide effect on objective_response, after adjustment for ECOG, age, albumin, LDH, hemoglobin, visceral mets, weight loss, and CRP.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ treatment_enzalutamide * (mcrpc + ar_v7_positive + brca2_mutation + msi_high) + clinical covariates)
analyses.result_summary: Term treatment_enzalutamide: coef=+2.6236, OR=13.785, p=0.00e+00.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ treatment_enzalutamide * (mcrpc + ar_v7_positive + brca2_mutation + msi_high) + clinical covariates)
analyses.result_summary: Term treatment_enzalutamide:mcrpc: coef=-2.2317, OR=0.107, p=0.00e+00.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ treatment_enzalutamide * (mcrpc + ar_v7_positive + brca2_mutation + msi_high) + clinical covariates)
analyses.result_summary: Term treatment_enzalutamide:ar_v7_positive: coef=-1.6432, OR=0.193, p=9.54e-140.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ treatment_enzalutamide * (mcrpc + ar_v7_positive + brca2_mutation + msi_high) + clinical covariates)
analyses.result_summary: Term treatment_enzalutamide:brca2_mutation: coef=-1.5522, OR=0.212, p=7.36e-67.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ treatment_enzalutamide * (mcrpc + ar_v7_positive + brca2_mutation + msi_high) + clinical covariates)
analyses.result_summary: Term treatment_enzalutamide:msi_high: coef=-1.6667, OR=0.189, p=1.65e-27.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL: treatment_enzalutamide produces a large positive treatment effect on objective_response (absolute difference ≈ +0.626; rr_treated ≈ 0.80 vs rr_untreated ≈ 0.17) in the joint subgroup defined by ALL of: mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0. Each of mCRPC, AR-V7+, BRCA2+, and MSI-high independently suppresses the effect; in patients with any of these features, enzalutamide does not increase objective_response.

FINAL: treatment_abiraterone shows no clinically meaningful effect on objective_response in any clinically defined subgroup tested (mCRPC, non-mCRPC, visceral mets, BRCA2 status, AR-V7 status, MSI status, PSMA status, or ECOG strata). All subgroup absolute differences |diff| < 0.025 with p > 0.05.
FINAL: treatment_docetaxel shows no clinically meaningful effect on objective_response in any clinically defined subgroup tested. All absolute differences |diff| < 0.01 with p > 0.5.

FINAL: treatment_olaparib shows no positive effect on objective_response in any tested subgroup, including the canonical BRCA2-mutated subgroup, where the direction is actually slightly NEGATIVE (rr_treated 0.121 vs rr_untreated 0.153, diff -0.032, p≈0.06). Hence the canonical olaparib-BRCA2 effect is NOT supported in this cohort.

FINAL: treatment_lu177_psma shows no positive effect on objective_response in any tested subgroup, including PSMA-high patients (diff = -0.001, p=0.88). In the visceral mets subgroup, lu177_psma shows a small NEGATIVE signal (diff = -0.024, p=0.04). The canonical Lu177-PSMA × PSMA-high benefit is NOT supported.

FINAL: treatment_pembrolizumab shows no significant effect on objective_response in any tested subgroup, including MSI-high patients (rr_treated 0.177 vs rr_untreated 0.176, diff +0.001, p=1.0). The canonical pembrolizumab × MSI-high benefit is NOT supported in this cohort.

analyses.code: Two-prop within (mcrpc=0 & ar_v7_positive=0 & brca2_mutation=0 & msi_high=0): objective_response by treatment_enzalutamide
analyses.result_summary: Inside the responder subgroup (n=15,681): treated rr=0.798 (n=6,325) vs untreated rr=0.172 (n=9,356); diff=+0.626, p≈0. Outside this subgroup (n=34,319): treated rr=0.161 (n=13,751) vs untreated rr=0.153 (n=20,568); diff=+0.008, p≈0.06. Adjusted interaction OR (treatment × responder-subgroup) = 18.98, p≈0.

analyses.code: Comprehensive subgroup search for each non-enzalutamide treatment.
analyses.result_summary: Across 15 prespecified subgroups for each of abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177_psma, and pembrolizumab, no subgroup showed a significant POSITIVE response improvement attributable to the treatment. Olaparib in BRCA2+ trended negative (diff=-0.032, p=0.058) and lu177_psma in visceral mets was significantly negative (diff=-0.024, p=0.043). Pembrolizumab in MSI-high (diff≈0, p=1.0) provided no benefit. None of these five drugs has a discoverable beneficial subgroup in this cohort.
